content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

March 6, 2001

Deborah A. Flores, Ph.D.
Santa Barbara School Districts
720 Santa Barbara Street
Santa Barbara, CA 93101

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-01-036

Dear Dr. Flores:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ As we discussed on February 22, 2001, our advice is
limited to the provisions of the Act. To the extent that other provisions of law may apply
to your situation, you will need to seek independent advice on these issues. Moreover,
because your questions are general in nature and do not reference a particular
governmental decision, we are issuing informal assistance.²

QUESTION

Does the Political Reform Act prohibit you from hiring Dr. Robert Pohl, a former
Santa Barbara Board of Education member, as an educational consultant for the school
district?

_________________________________
¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18996, of the California Code of Regulations.
² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Government Code section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3), a copy of which is
enclosed.)

File No. I-01-036
Page No. 2

CONCLUSION

You may not make any decision, including a decision to hire Dr. Pohl, if it is
reasonably foreseeable that this decision will have a material financial effect on your
financial interests. The Act does not prohibit the contract itself.

FACTS

You are the superintendent for the Santa Barbara school districts. You would like
to hire Dr. Robert Pohl, a former member of the Santa Barbara Board of Education, as a
consultant to revise the evaluation process for school principals and to work with under-
performing schools to develop strategies for improvement. Dr. Pohl is an educational
consultant by profession and has extensive experience working with schools, particularly
under-performing schools. In our telephone conversation of February 22, 2001, you
clarified that you are contemplating that the contract with Dr. Pohl will be a one-year
contract. You also stated that as a board member, Dr. Pohl received per diem from the
district.

ANALYSIS

A public official may not make, participate in making, or in any way attempt to
use his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the official
knows or has reason to know, that he or she has a financial interest. (Section 87100.)
The conflict-of-interest provisions cover specific conduct: making, participating in
making or attempting to use your official position to influence a governmental decision.
(Section 87100.) If you hire Dr. Pohl this would be considered making a governmental
decision and would therefore be regulated by the Act. (Regulation 18702.1(a)(3).)

The conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act apply only to conflicts arising from
economic interests. (Regulation 18703.) The Commission has enumerated several
economic interests that have the potential to cause a disqualifying conflict of interest.
(Section 87103(a) – (e).)

Because the Act applies only to conflicts of interest arising from economic
interests, merely making a decision to hire a former board member would not cause a
disqualifying conflict of interest unless you have an economic interest in that person. You
have not provided any information about your own economic interests; thus, we cannot
fully analyze this issue. However, so long as this decision would not have a material
financial effect on your economic interests, you are free to make, participate in making or
influence this governmental decision.³


³ If Dr. Pohl is employed by the district this may raise other issues under the Act. The Commission
has included some consultants within the definition of "public official" and may therefore be regulated by
the Act. You should contact us if you believe that Dr. Pohl will be a "consultant" under the Act.

File No. I-01-036
Page No. 3

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
Acting General Counsel

By: Melissa Mikesell
Legal Intern

MAM:jg
Enclosures
